title: US imposes sanctions on DR Congo ex-President Kabila alleging rebel support

originalTitle: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
title: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
content: The zero-tariff regime gives China's soft power a boost, but may lead to uneven gains, say analysts.

originalTitle: Man sentenced to death for murder of toddlers at Ugandan nursery
title: Keeping Everyone Safe: A Happy Ending for Children in Uganda!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! It’s your friendly news reporter here, ready to share some important news from our lovely country, Uganda. Today, we are talking about how grown-ups work very hard to keep all children safe and happy. This is a story about justice and how our community looks after each other.

What Happened?

Once upon a time, there was a school in Uganda where very little children, like toddlers, went to learn and play. They were happy and safe. But one day, a man named Christopher Okello Onyum did a very, very bad thing to some of these little children. It was a very sad day for everyone. When someone does something wrong, like hurting others, it is very important that they are stopped. And that is exactly what happened. The police, who are like superheroes for safety, caught the man.

Going to Court

When something bad happens, there is a special place where people go to talk about it. It is called a court. In the court, people listen to what happened, and they try to understand everything. It’s like a very big meeting where everyone tries to find out the truth. In this court, the man, Christopher, said that he was very, very sick in his mind and didn't know what he was doing. But a very wise grown-up called a judge listened to everything very carefully. The judge is like a super-teacher who makes sure everyone follows the rules and that fairness happens.

The Judge's Important Decision

The judge listened to all the stories and looked at all the evidence. Evidence means all the clues and facts that help us understand what happened. After thinking very, very hard, the judge made a big decision. The judge said that the man was not sick in the way he said. The judge knew that what he did was very wrong. So, the judge made sure that Christopher Okello Onyum will not be able to hurt anyone ever again. He will stay away for a very, very long time. This is how the court makes sure that bad things stop and that everyone, especially children, can be safe. **Did you know?** Judges wear special robes in court. This is to show that they are very important and fair when they make decisions!

Why is This Important?

This story is important because it shows us that grown-ups, like police officers, teachers, and judges, work together to keep us safe. Our schools and homes should always be places where we feel happy and protected. When someone breaks the rules and hurts others, there are brave people who will step in to make things right. This makes sure that schools are safe places for all children to learn, play, and make friends. It means that parents can feel happy when they send their children to school, knowing they are in good hands and protected by the law.

Who Helps Us Stay Safe?

Many people in our community help us stay safe. Police officers are brave men and women who protect us from danger. Teachers at school look after us and teach us important lessons, not just from books, but about being kind and safe. Our parents, grandparents, and other family members also work very hard to keep us out of harm's way. Everyone has a part to play in making our homes, schools, and villages safe and happy places for everyone. **Did you know?** Uganda is sometimes called the 'Pearl of Africa' because it is so beautiful and has so many wonderful animals and places to see!

Rules Keep Us Safe

Just like in our games, where we have rules to make sure everyone has fun and plays fair, our country has rules too. These rules are called laws. Laws are very important because they help everyone live together peacefully and safely. When someone breaks a law, like hurting another person, the grown-ups in charge, like the judge, make sure there is a fair consequence. These consequences are not just to punish the person who did wrong, but also to show everyone that breaking the rules has serious results. And most importantly, it helps keep everyone else safe for the future.

Feeling Safe in Uganda

Uganda is a beautiful country with kind people. It's important to remember that bad things don't happen often, and there are many, many more good people than bad. This story shows us that even when something sad happens, the system works to make sure justice is served and children are protected. We should always remember to be kind to each other, to look out for our friends, and to tell a trusted grown-up if we ever feel unsafe or see something that worries us. That is how we all help keep our communities strong and safe. **Did you know?** There are over 50 different languages spoken in Uganda, but English is also spoken by many people to help everyone understand each other!

A Message for All Children

Dear children, you are precious and special. Your safety is the most important thing. Always remember that there are many grown-ups who love you and want to keep you safe. If you ever feel scared or worried, please talk to a parent, a teacher, a police officer, or any grown-up you trust. They are there to help you. This news story tells us that when someone tries to hurt children, the good grown-ups will always step in to make sure that person cannot hurt anyone again. So, you can feel safe and happy, knowing that your community cares deeply about you. Keep playing, keep learning, and keep smiling! Until next time, stay safe and happy, my friends!
summary: A man in Uganda who hurt little children at a school has been stopped by a judge, ensuring he will not be able to hurt anyone again and keeping children safe.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that grown-ups like judges and police work very hard to keep all children safe, and bad people who hurt others will be stopped so everyone can live happily and without fear.

title: Leeds beat Burnley to move closer to safety
